proven.claim: PROVEN: totalSignals >= fileCount at the time of the log.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 97
; PROVEN: totalSignals >= fileCount at the time of the log.
; fileCount increments only when signals.length > 0, adding at least 1 to totalSignals.
(declare-const total_signals Int)
(declare-const file_count Int)
(declare-const n Int)  ; number of counted files
(declare-const s Int)  ; minimum signals per counted file
; Code transition: s >= 1 (guard: `if (signals.length > 0)`)
(assert (>= s 1))
; Code transition: file_count = n; total_signals >= n * s (each counted file contributes >= 1)
(assert (>= n 0))
(assert (= file_count n))
(assert (>= total_signals (* n s)))
; Negated property: totalSignals < fileCount
(assert (< total_signals file_count))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: fileCount cannot exceed the number of files iterated
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 97
; PROVEN: fileCount cannot exceed the number of files iterated
(declare-const ts_files_len Int)
(declare-const file_count Int)
; Code transition: loop iterates exactly ts_files_len times; fileCount increments at most once per iteration
(assert (>= ts_files_len 0))
(assert (>= file_count 0))
(assert (<= file_count ts_files_len))
; Negated property: fileCount > tsFiles.length
(assert (> file_count ts_files_len))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: fileCount counts only files where signals.length > 0, not total files scanned.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 97
; VIOLATION: fileCount counts only files where signals.length > 0, not total files scanned.
; "Found X signals across Y files" is semantically misleading — Y undercounts coverage.
(declare-const files_scanned Int)
(declare-const files_with_signals Int)
(declare-const files_without_signals Int)
; Code transition: files_scanned = files_with_signals + files_without_signals
(assert (= files_scanned (+ files_with_signals files_without_signals)))
(assert (>= files_with_signals 0))
(assert (>= files_without_signals 0))
; Code transition: fileCount = files_with_signals (guard: signals.length > 0)
; Violation: files_without_signals > 0 → "Y files" < actual scanned file count
(assert (> files_without_signals 0))
(assert (> files_scanned files_with_signals))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: tsFiles has no deduplication. If the same resolved path appears twice
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 97
; VIOLATION: tsFiles has no deduplication. If the same resolved path appears twice
; (e.g., from symlinks or overlapping git-ls-files matches), both iterations fire,
; and both totalSignals and fileCount are inflated.
(declare-const path_a Int)              ; symbolic ID for first occurrence
(declare-const path_b Int)              ; symbolic ID for second occurrence
(declare-const signals_per_file Int)
(declare-const total_signals Int)
(declare-const file_count Int)
; Code transition: both array entries resolve to the same absolute path
(assert (= path_a path_b))
; Code transition: both iterations succeed and pass the signals.length > 0 guard
(assert (> signals_per_file 0))
; Code transition: totalSignals and fileCount incremented for each occurrence independently
(assert (= total_signals (* 2 signals_per_file)))
(assert (= file_count 2))
; Violation: totalSignals and fileCount are both inflated — same file counted twice
(assert (> total_signals signals_per_file))
(check-sat)
